spec_match.rationale: Six data: URL SVGs with no id on the root, two modes (bare URL vs #root) — exact mapping of the v137 change.

interactive.rationale: Two-mode toggle, live re-render of the grid, JS probe that reports detection state.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Feature index promises six icons with no id, toggleable between bare and fragment — page does exactly that.

edge_cases.rationale: Doesn't show an SVG that DOES have id="root" (where both modes succeed), or one with nested target ids, or how the new behaviour interacts with <symbol>.

references.rationale: Correct chromestatus 5128141573718016 link and SVG2 spec are both present.

design_system.rationale: All page styling via tokens, .alt buttons explicitly invert background + color together avoiding the global button trap.

openQuestions.title: Add an explicit-id sprite lane
openQuestions.detail: Show a row with an icon SVG that has id="root" on its root element — both bare-URL and #root should resolve, making it clear the v137 change is additive.

openQuestions.title: Demonstrate the still-required-fragment case
openQuestions.detail: If the SVG file contains multiple top-level <symbol> definitions, bare-URL picks the root <svg> wrapper, not a specific symbol. Show that so authors know when they still need a fragment.

summary: Strong, focused before-after demo with a clean probe. Gaps are on the edge-case side.
